mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor demand, triggering risk-off positioning and negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are not significantly negative when UNRATE crosses 4.5%, or the signal produces false breakouts exceeding 40% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real personal consumption expenditures indicates a synchronized demand-supply mismatch that compresses corporate margins and depresses equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows statistically insignificant coefficients.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, rising housing starts amplify the sensitivity of consumer credit to borrowing costs, accelerating consumption slowdowns and driving negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: HOUST increases do not correlate with negative SPX returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the conditional forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP consistently drives earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter as corporate cash flows expand and investor confidence rises.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no predictive power for 60-day forward returns, or the relationship reverses sign during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls fall below 100,000 for two consecutive months while the unemployment rate remains below 4.0%, it signals a hidden labor market softening that precedes equity drawdowns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal does not outperform a baseline UNRATE-only signal in predicting negative returns, or forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production coinciding with elevated inflation readings indicates supply-driven price pressures that force the central bank to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction between INDPRO and CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative returns, or high inflation with strong production leads to positive equity returns due to nominal growth expectations.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid employment growth, rising housing starts act as a leading indicator for durable goods consumption, driving positive equity returns over the following week as consumer spending accelerates.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns when PAYEMS exceeds 200,000, or the signal produces significant drawdowns in more than 50% of instances.
